explanation: This command uses PowerShell to add a Defender exclusion path. It includes quotes around the command line and uses the full cmdlet name, which aligns with the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses the short form of the -Command parameter (-c) and sets a Defender exclusion for a process. It matches the Sigma rule's detection of Set-MpPreference with exclusion parameters.
This command adds an exclusion for a file extension using Add-MpPreference. The use of quotes and escaped double quotes demonstrates safe mutation techniques while maintaining the original intent.
This command sets an exclusion for an IP address using Set-MpPreference. It uses the -Command parameter and includes quotes around the command line, aligning with the Sigma rule's detection criteria.

This command uses an escaped quote at the end of the path to demonstrate safe mutation techniques. The command remains valid and matches the Sigma rule's detection of Add-MpPreference with exclusion parameters.
